canonical_title: Anthropic donates another $20 million to Public First Action for AI regulation advocacy
aliases: Anthropic $20 million AI regulation lobbying donation
treatment: Treat as a corporate-policy and lobbying-spend topic. Reinclude only for additional material donations, disclosed campaign deployment, binding policy influence, legal restrictions, or a concrete response from rival AI political groups.
known_facts: Anthropic announced an additional $20 million donation to Public First Action on 2026-07-22, following a separate $20 million contribution in February 2026.
Public First Action advocates for mitigating major AI risks and supports political action committees aligned with stricter AI regulation.
Anthropic stated that the donation cannot be used to influence the election of any federal, state, or local candidate.

Public First Action was created to counter Leading the Future, a group that generally opposes stricter AI regulation and is backed by figures including OpenAI president Greg Brockman and Marc Andreessen.
reinclude_only_if: Anthropic or Public First Action discloses a material new donation or specific policy campaign funded by the contribution.
A regulator, court, election authority, or legislature imposes a binding rule or disclosure requirement affecting AI-company political spending.
Leading the Future, OpenAI executives, or other major AI companies announce a concrete counter-campaign or materially larger political expenditure.
Credible reporting establishes a direct policy outcome attributable to this spending.
notes: Reuters published the report at 2026-07-22 09:03 UTC, or 18:03 JST, inside the night-edition coverage window. This is a policy-influence and corporate-governance delta, not a model or product release.
